questions.q: V.V. Giri served as which President of India?
questions.explanation: Varahagiri Venkata Giri was the fourth President of India.

questions.q: V.V. Giri is uniquely noted as the only person to be elected President of India in what capacity?
questions.explanation: He was the only person elected President of India as an independent candidate.

questions.q: Before entering high constitutional office, V.V. Giri was prominently associated with which movement?
questions.explanation: He was a prominent trade union leader, earning the title 'the Labour Leader President'.

questions.q: In which year did V.V. Giri become the elected President of India?
questions.explanation: He was elected President of India in 1969.

questions.q: In which year did V.V. Giri's tenure as President of India end?
questions.explanation: His term as President ended in 1974.

questions.q: Which office did V.V. Giri hold immediately before being elected President?
questions.explanation: He was Vice-President of India before being elected President in 1969.

questions.q: V.V. Giri's popular epithet reflects his association with which group?
questions.explanation: He is remembered as the Labour Leader President for his trade union work.

questions.q: V.V. Giri served as the constitutional head of which country?
questions.explanation: He served as the President of the Republic of India.

questions.q: Whom did V.V. Giri succeed as the elected President of India?
questions.explanation: He was elected after the death in office of President Zakir Husain.

questions.q: What do the initials 'V.V.' in V.V. Giri's name stand for?
questions.explanation: V.V. Giri's full name is Varahagiri Venkata Giri.

questions.q: Before the 1969 presidential election, V.V. Giri first served briefly as Acting President because of which event?
questions.explanation: As Vice-President he became Acting President after Zakir Husain died in office in 1969.

questions.q: V.V. Giri served as Vice-President of India under which President?
questions.explanation: He was Vice-President from 1967 to 1969 during the presidency of Zakir Husain.

questions.q: Who succeeded V.V. Giri as President of India in 1974?
questions.explanation: Fakhruddin Ali Ahmed became the fifth President of India in 1974.

questions.q: Besides Vice-President, V.V. Giri also held which Union ministerial portfolio earlier in his career?
questions.explanation: He served as Union Minister for Labour, in keeping with his trade union background.

questions.q: V.V. Giri was awarded India's highest civilian honour. Which one?
questions.explanation: He was awarded the Bharat Ratna, India's highest civilian honour, in 1975.

questions.q: Before becoming Vice-President, V.V. Giri served as Governor of more than one state, including which one?
questions.explanation: He served as Governor of Uttar Pradesh, among other states, before becoming Vice-President.

questions.q: In which year did V.V. Giri become Vice-President of India?
questions.explanation: He was elected Vice-President in 1967, the same year Zakir Husain became President.

questions.q: As Vice-President, V.V. Giri served as ex-officio Chairman of which House of Parliament?
questions.explanation: The Vice-President is the ex-officio Chairman of the Rajya Sabha.

questions.q: V.V. Giri's presidency from 1969 to 1974 coincided with the tenure of which Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: His presidency overlapped with Indira Gandhi's tenure as Prime Minister.

questions.q: To contest the 1969 presidential election, V.V. Giri resigned from which office he then held?
questions.explanation: He resigned as Acting President (the Vice-Presidential office) to contest as an independent candidate.

questions.q: Which state of India is associated with V.V. Giri's birth and early life?
questions.explanation: He was born in Berhampur in present-day Odisha.

questions.q: V.V. Giri pursued his higher legal studies in which country before returning to India?
questions.explanation: He studied law in Ireland, where he was also influenced by political movements of the time.

questions.q: In which year was V.V. Giri born?
questions.explanation: He was born on 10 August 1894.

questions.q: In which year did V.V. Giri pass away?
questions.explanation: He died in 1980, some years after completing his presidency.

questions.q: V.V. Giri was once associated with the presidency of which major national labour federation?
options: The Federation of Indian Chambers of Commerce
The All India Trade Union Congress
questions.explanation: He was associated with the leadership of the All India Trade Union Congress as a labour leader.

questions.q: In his university studies in Ireland, V.V. Giri attended an institution in which city?
questions.explanation: He studied at University College Dublin in Ireland.

questions.q: Apart from Uttar Pradesh, V.V. Giri also served as Governor of which southern state?
questions.explanation: He served as Governor of Kerala before his later national offices.

questions.q: Early in his career, V.V. Giri served as India's High Commissioner to which country?
questions.explanation: He served as India's High Commissioner to Ceylon, now Sri Lanka, in the late 1940s.

questions.q: The national institute named after V.V. Giri, established to study labour issues, is located in which city?
questions.explanation: The V.V. Giri National Labour Institute is located in Noida, near Delhi.

questions.q: Under the Constitution, the President's office that V.V. Giri held has a fixed term of how many years?
questions.explanation: The President of India holds office for a term of five years under the Constitution.
